communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: FRS Reports (George Mason University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request the NCAA Membership Financial Reporting System revenue and expense reports for fiscal (or reporting) years 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023 & 2024. Specifically:

These reports are completed by the athletic department for the National Collegiate Athletic Association for the fiscal years listed above. This report is a multi-page document that had to be submitted to the NCAA in January. It contains 40+ revenue and expense categories, followed by specific breakdowns of each of those categories, by sport and gender. I am requesting the full report, including the detail tables, the “Other Reporting Items” list and the “Statement of Revenues and Expenses” that appears at the end of the report. PLEASE NOTE: The NCAA report is different than the equity report that is sent to the U.S. Department of Education for Title IX compliance.

I prefer to receive the records electronically as an Adobe Acrobat (.pdf) file.

If you choose to deny this request, please provide a written explanation for the denial including a reference to the specific statutory exemption(s) on which you rely. Also, please provide all segregable portions of otherwise exempt material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Stacey Brook.

title: Medford Police (OR) - Flock Camera policy, invoice, map, and contracts

communications.subject: Oregon Public Records Law Request: Medford Police (OR) - Flock Camera policy, invoice, map, and contracts
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Oregon Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the records previously produced by the Medford Police and the City of Medford under records P015141-010726. 

(1) all policies the City of Medford, Oregon has that oversee and/or regulate the operation and use of Automated License Plate Readers, including the operation and use of Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares
(2) a copy of any and all invoices Medford has between any and all Automated License Plate Readers softwares and programs, including Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares
(3) a map of all ALPR cameras that Medford owns, operates or has access to
(4) a copy of any and all license and user agreements, contracts, memorandums of understanding or other similar type documents that describe and/or regulate the use, retention, collection, and sharing of ALPR data including but not limited to the agreements between Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares

By requesting previously produced records, I am hopeful that this request will require minimal labor to locate, review and produce the records. Please let me know if you have any questions.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Southern Oregon Flock Off

communications.subject: Police Records Request :: P016327-040526
communications.communication:
Dear Southern Oregon Flock Off: 
Thank you for your interest in public records of City of Medford (the "City"). Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the Oregon Public Records Law. Your request was received by the City on 4/5/2026 and given the reference number P016327-040526 for tracking purposes. 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant City department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. At this time, it is uncertain if the City is in fact the "custodian of record" for the document(s) you seek. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question at a later time. PLEASE NOTE: The Oregon Public Records Law does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
 Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Oregon Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records: 

A copy of the records previously produced by the Medford Police and the City of Medford under records P015141-010726. 

(1) all policies the City of Medford, Oregon has that oversee and/or regulate the operation and use of Automated License Plate Readers, including the operation and use of Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares 
(2) a copy of any and all invoices Medford has between any and all Automated License Plate Readers softwares and programs, including Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares 
(3) a map of all ALPR cameras that Medford owns, operates or has access to 
(4) a copy of any and all license and user agreements, contracts, memorandums of understanding or other similar type documents that describe and/or regulate the use, retention, collection, and sharing of ALPR data including but not limited to the agreements between Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares 

By requesting previously produced records, I am hopeful that this request will require minimal labor to locate, review and produce the records. Please let me know if you have any questions. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days. 

Sincerely, 

Southern Oregon Flock Off 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2258295/ 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Public Records Center. 
 
Thank you, 
City of Medford

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Less-Lethals Restock (Los Angeles Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Hello, 

I am requesting records related to your department's purchase and use of "less lethals" 
Copies of purchase records, purchase requests, invoices, and any documents from vendors related to the purchase of for

- less lethal weaponry (launchers, pepperball guns, any CO2 powered weaponry) 
- less lethal ammo (40mm rounds, 37mm baton rounds, pepperballs and marking rounds) 
-  tear gas, CS gas, CN gas, pepper spray, distraction devices, and other tossable canisters
- Any documentation related to the number of the above munitions/rounds/canisters used 
- Any memos, emails, directives, or other official orders related to the use of force at the ongoing protests, including documentation of injuries

Please search June 6th to June 12th, 2025

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

communications.subject: [External Message Added] City of Los Angeles public records request #25-8386
communications.communication:
City of Los Angeles ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #25-8386: Dear Requester, The Department has reviewed your California Public Records Act request. Please be advised that, pursuant to California Government Code Section 7922.535, the Department has found that “unusual circumstances” exist with respect to the request due to the need to search for, collect, and review the requested records from other Department entities which are separate from the office processing the request. Therefore, our staff will require the statutory fourteen days extension of time in which to respond. communications.subject: [External Message Added] City of Los Angeles public records request #25-8386
communications.communication:
City of Los Angeles ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #25-8386: Dear Requester, The Los Angeles Police Department (“LAPD” or “Department”) has reviewed your request under the California Public Records Act (Cal. Govt. Code section 7920.000, et seq., hereinafter the “CPRA” or “Act”) seeking the following records: “…records related to your department's purchase and use of "less lethals" 1-Copies of purchase records, purchase requests, invoices, and any documents from vendors related to the purchase of for - less lethal weaponry (launchers, pepperball guns, any CO2 powered weaponry) - less lethal ammo (40mm rounds, 37mm baton rounds, pepperballs and marking rounds) - tear gas, CS gas, CN gas, pepper spray, distraction devices, and other tossable canisters 2- Any documentation related to the number of the above munitions/rounds/canisters used 3- Any memos, emails, directives, or other official orders related to the use of force at the ongoing protests, including documentation of injuries Please search June 6th to June 12th, 2025” The Department is cognizant of its responsibilities under the Act. It recognizes the statutory scheme was enacted to maximize citizen access to the workings of government. The Act does not mandate disclosure of all documents within the government’s possession. Rather, by specific exemption and reference to other statutes, the Act recognizes that there are boundaries where the public’s right to access must be balanced against such weighty considerations as the right of privacy, a right of constitutional dimension under California Constitution, Article 1, Section 1. The law also exempts from disclosure records that are privileged or confidential or otherwise exempt under either express provisions of the Act or pursuant to applicable federal or state law, per California Government Code Sections 7927.200; 7927.700; 7923.600; 7927.705; and 7922.000. The Department continues to search for records responsive to item one on your request and will provide you with a determination in September 2025. As for item two on your request, the Department conducted a search and currently there are no existing records responsive to your request. Please note that to the extent that any kinetic energy projectiles or chemical agents were used these records will be posted on the Department’s official website, pursuant to the Assembly Bill 48, to when they become available, and can be accessed at the following link: https://www.lapdonline.org/ab48/ Lastly, the Department is currently conducting a search for records responsive to your request for Use of Force and will provide you with a determination in September 2025. However, based on the information provided, we are unable to conduct a search for email communications. Please provide additional information (i.e. names, serial numbers, email addresses, etc.) to assist with the search. Please respond by no later than July 14, 2025, if you wish to proceed. If we do not receive a response by the date indicated, we will consider this item on your request closed. If you have any questions, please respond to this email. Respectfully, LAPD Public Records & Subpoena Response Section, CPRA Unit ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at City of Los Angeles.

Technical support: See our help page

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: 2026 Ohio (Secretary of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern:
Pursuant to the Ohio Freedom of Information Law and the new 2026 Ohio Sunshine Laws Manual I hereby request the following records:
Pursuant to Section 149.43 of the Ohio Revised Code, I hereby request the following records:  unofficial copies of any documents of complaints and actions taken (any info allowable by law) filed with the Ohio Charitable Law and the Ohio Secretary of State regarding :


Congregation Sholom
DBA: Temple Sholom of Cincinnati (doing business as that has lapsed)
10828 Kenwood Rd. Cincinnati, OH 45242
EIN 31-0584318


Central Conference of American Rabbis - OH Registration #26991
EIN: 13-1769747
NY State Registration Number - 05-99-38
Recently changed address:
183 Madison Ave #1504
New York, NY 10016
Address listed with Charities Bureau:
355 LEXINGTON AVE 8th Floor, null
NEW YORK , NY 10017

Ohio Subsidiary
Ohio Entity ID 26991
1160 Dublin Road, Suite 400
Columbus OH 43215
Pursuant to Section 149.43 of the Ohio Revised Code & New York Public Officers Law § 87(2), I request access to and copies of all non-exempt public records in the possession, custody, or control of the Ohio Attorney General or any other public office relating to the Central Conference of American Rabbis & Temple Sholom (Cincinnati, Ohio), including but not limited to records connected to any complaints, investigations, or actions taken under Ohio Charitable Law.
If any portion of the requested records is withheld, I request that all reasonably segregable, non-exempt portions be provided, with redactions. 
This request specifically includes, and is not limited to:
All copies of any and all organizational and governing documents submitted to, filed with, or otherwise in the possession of any public office relating to Congregation Sholom (Temple Sholom of Cincinnati) and the Central Conference of American Rabbis, including but not limited to articles of incorporation or association, bylaws, constitutions, amendments, and any other governance or structural documents.
All complaints submitted against Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis and its representatives, attorneys, or agents, submitted to any public office, including responses to complaints, position statements, and supporting exhibits.
All documents, correspondence, and materials submitted by Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis and its representatives, attorneys, or agents to any public office, including responses to complaints, position statements, and supporting exhibits
All communications between Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis  (or its representatives) and any public office, including emails, letters, requests for information, follow-up communications, and meeting summaries.
All documents, records, or materials that were reviewed, relied upon, cited, or considered by any public office in connection with complaints, inquiries, or investigations involving Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis regardless of whether such materials originated from Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis or third parties.
All records created by any public office relating to Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis including but not limited to investigative summaries, factual findings, memoranda, recommendations, determinations, closure letters, or case status communications.
Any financial, contractual, or funding-related records involving Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis that were submitted to or reviewed by a public office, including any materials referencing grants, loans, or other funding (including but not limited to COVID-related funding) if such materials were part of any complaint, review, or investigation.
All complaints and supporting documentation submitted by third parties relating to Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis including any attachments or exhibits.
All final or closed-case materials, sanctions, settlements, assurance of discontinuance agreements, administrative subpoenas, court filings, court orders, closure letters and determination summaries.
Any Foia Requests made to the Attorney General for information on Temple Sholom or Central Conference of American Rabbis, the date of the request, the name of the person making the request, and the responsive documents provided.
I request that I be properly classified as a news media requester. I am a complainant and a writer for the Times of Israel and I am requesting this information in that editorial capacity.  The Times of Israel is widely recognized for its reporting, publishing regular features on issues of public interest and importance.  The information may also be used in a documentary that is currently being filed.  You may view my articles at the address listed:  https://blogs.timesofisrael.com/author/julie-guilfoyle/
If you have any questions about my media status, please contact me.
In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request be filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.  If there is a large cost, then as an alternative, I request for the records to be made available for inspection.
Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request.
Sincerely,
Julie Guilfoyle

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Julie Guilfoyle

communications.communication:
Ms. Guilfoyle,

This email is in response to your records request for all records for Congregation Sholom and Central Conference of American Rabbis. Your request comingles documents regarding consumer complaints that might be in the possession of the Ohio Attorney General's office, or New York governmental agencies. The Ohio Secretary of State is a business registrar, so our production is limited to those business entity filings. We did, however, review whether there were any fraudulent business filing complaints with these organizations, but did not find any responsive records. In fulfillment of your request, please find attached the responsive business records.


[logo for the Office of Frank LaRose Ohio Secretary of State]
Jason Boylan | Senior Legal Counsel

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Records Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request access to and copies of all records related to the procurement of a school bus stop-arm camera / school bus infraction detection system associated with: 

Title/Description: School Bus Cloud-Based Camera System

Issuing Entity: Ferndale Public Schools 

Specifically, I am requesting: 

All proposals and responses submitted by all vendors. 

All contracts, agreements, amendments, addenda, and pricing schedules resulting from this solicitation. 

All evaluation materials, including scoring sheets, evaluation committee notes or summaries, and recommendation memoranda. 

All bid tabulations, comparison matrices, or award justifications. 

All addenda to the solicitation, and any questions and answers (Q&A) or clarifications issued to vendors. 

Any other documentation or correspondence (including emails, letters, or internal memoranda) material to the evaluation, selection, and award of this stop-arm camera enforcement program.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jon Smith

communications.subject: FOIA Request
communications.communication:
This will acknowledge receipt of your letter received January 20, 2026,
wherein you requested information pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of
Information Act.



Please be advised that the City of Danbury is firmly committed to providing
prompt access to all records subject to disclosure under Connecticut
General Statutes §1-210, et. seq.  Accordingly, all documents will be
assembled and reviewed as quickly as reasonably possible in light of other
responsibilities and immediate commitments.  Although the City of Danbury
takes your request seriously and will respond as promptly as possible, we
reserve the right to petition the Freedom of Information Commission for a
declaratory ruling regarding requests that may be exempt from disclosure.



Please note, that there will be a charge of $.50 per page, and any amount
of over $10.00 must be paid in advance.



 If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact our office.


[image: photo]
*Kimberly Anderson*
Legal Administrative Assistant
Office of Corporation Counsel
City of Danbury

(203) 797-4518 ext. 7212 | k.anderson@danbury-ct.gov

(203) 796-8043 Fax

155 Deer Hill Avenue, Danbury, CT 06810

This request is now in a state of "Deemed Denial" under CGS § 1-206(a), as more than four business days have passed without a production of records or a valid legal explanation for their withholding.

Please be advised that per the Connecticut Freedom of Information Commission's strict 30-day jurisdictional window, I will be filing a formal Notice of Appeal if these records are not received by February 13, 2026. I would prefer to resolve this via this portal, but I must act to preserve my state-mandated appeal rights.

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (SAU70)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)
We are working on this request.

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Records Request (Henry County Public Schools)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request access to and copies of all records related to the procurement of all school bus stop-arm camera contracts and amendments which includes the following:

-The total number of buses in their fleet;
-The total number of buses serving special needs students;
-The total number of buses installed with a stop arm camera system; and
-The total number of buses serving special needs students installed with a stop arm camera system.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to you acknowledgement.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jon Smith

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Records Request (Henry County Public Schools)
communications.communication:
Good morning,
This is acknowledgement of your records requests dated April 1, 2026. You requested the following:

of all records related to the procurement of all school bus stop-arm camera contracts and amendments which includes the following:

-The total number of buses in their fleet;
-The total number of buses serving special needs students;
-The total number of buses installed with a stop arm camera system; and
-The total number of buses serving special needs students installed with a stop arm camera system.
We ask for the following clarification:

  1.  What is your intended use of the information?
Henry County Schools provides the following response at this time:
Legal Compliance will complete an initial evaluation of your records request.
Note: Your request will be evaluated to determine whether any responsive record(s) exist; whether any responsive record(s) are specifically exempted from disclosure by law; the timeline for non-exempted responsive record(s) production including a good faith effort to retrieve requested records; the method of any non-exempted responsive record(s) production; any charges for the search, retrieval, redaction, and production or copying costs for the production of any non-exempted responsive record(s); and any additional fee for the copying of such records or data not to exceed 10 cent(s) per page or the actual cost of producing the copy, among other permissible considerations. Typical costs and/or fees amount to $25.00 or less; however, if it appears that costs to respond to your request are in excess of $25.00, we will notify you within a reasonable amount of time and inform you of the estimate of the costs.
Have a good day.
Catina


Catina M. Fenner, M.S., CPM
Coordinator of Legal Compliance |  Henry County Schools<http://www.henry.k12.ga.us/>

title: FOIA J. Burney - US Army - Vietnam War

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA J. Burney - US Army - Vietnam War
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Name of Veteran: Jerry L. Burney
Branch: United States Army
DOB: March 6, 1944
Dates of Service: 1965 to 1988

Please provide the following military service information:
1. dates of service, dates of ranks, stations, list of awards, decorations, Badges, assignments, Vietnam War service
2. Section VII CURRENT AND PREVIOUS ASSIGNMENTS form
3. copy of Bronze Star Medal award citation
4. Military Education/ training courses
5. copy of Defense Meritorious Service Medal award citation
6. request copy of Army commendation award citations
7. Section V MISCELLANEOUS Form

Historical Documentation
military career include Army Commendation Medal, Republic of Vietnam Gallantry Cross Unit Citation Ribbon, Vietnam Service Medal.

First name: Jerry
Last name: Burney
DOB - 3/6/1944

I request hard copies of paperwork in the file showing this information. 
Please mail hardcopies to:

MUCKROCK NEWS
263 HUNTINGTON AVE
BOSTON, MA 02115

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley

title: Student data purchases and licensing agreements

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Student data purchases and licensing agreements
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

1) All active contracts and statements of works between the university and College Board, ACT Inc., EAB Inc., and/or Technolutions (also known as Slate.org) for the provision of the following goods or services:

- The purchase, licensing, or other use of data about high school students. This includes student name list buys and subscriptions to prospective student search services.
- The creation of advertising audience segments or profiles of high school students for the purpose of marketing campaigns.
- The creation and/or execution of digital marketing campaigns targeting high school students.

2) All invoices and purchase orders for high school student data purchased and/or licensed by the university since Jan. 1, 2023 from College Board, ACT, EAB, and/or Technolutions. This should include any instructions given to the vendor(s) regarding filters applied to the student data (e.g. limiting the data to students from certain geographic areas,  with particular GPAs, or intended majors). 

3) All invoices and purchase orders  between the university and College Board, ACT, EAB, and/or Technolutions since Jan. 1, 2023 for the creation of advertising audience segments or profiles. This should include any instructions given to the vendor(s) regarding the parameters of the audience segments or profiles (e.g. audiences who have visited certain websites or whose estimated income is within a certain range).

4) All invoices and purchase orders between the university and College Board, ACT, EAB, and/or Technolutions since Jan. 1, 2023 for targeted digital advertising campaigns. This should include instructions given to the vendor(s) regarding the parameters of those campaigns (e.g. the dates the campaigns should run, what platforms they should run on, and how many impressions are included in the campaigns)

If you have any questions about this request, please don't hesitate to ask.

If you choose to redact portions of responsive documents, I ask that you cite the statutory exemptions and associated case law underlying your decision to withhold each portion from public review.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Todd Feathers

communications.communication:
Dear Sir, Madam:

Thank you for contacting the Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) Office at University at Albany.

In regards to your email  dated 1/27/2025 for multiple Foil requests for records related to HS data:
“1) All active contracts and statements of works between the university and College Board, ACT Inc., EAB Inc., and/or Technolutions (also known as Slate.org) for the provision of the following goods or services:

2) All invoices and purchase orders for high school student data purchased and/or licensed by the university since Jan. 1, 2023 from College Board, ACT, EAB, and/or Technolutions.

3) All invoices and purchase orders between the university and College Board, ACT, EAB, and/or Technolutions since Jan. 1, 2023 for the creation of advertising audience segments or profiles.
4) All invoices and purchase orders between the university and College Board, ACT, EAB, and/or Technolutions since Jan. 1, 2023 for targeted digital advertising campaigns”

Please note that we are still reviewing your requests. We expect to have a determination as to whether or not the University at Albany has any records responsive to any of your requests within 90 days (by 7/2/2025). We will also let you know by that the responsive records (if any) submission date.

Kind regards,

Foil@albany.edu<mailto:Foil@albany.edu>

Corinne Fauchon |  FOIL Records Access Officer

Office of Enterprise Risk Management and Compliance

University at Albany | State University of New York at Albany [cid:image001.jpg@01DB86CA.DD8BBD00]

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Student data purchases and licensing agreements
communications.communication:
Dear Requestor:

I am writing to follow up on your FOIL request dated January 27th, 2025, for the following:


  1.  All active contracts and statements of works between the university and College Board, ACT Inc., EAB Inc., and/or Technolutions (also known as Slate.org)…;
  2.  All invoices and purchase orders for high school student data purchased and/or licensed by the university since Jan. 1, 2023 from College Board, ACT, EAB, and/or Technolutions;
  3.  All invoices and purchase orders between the university and College Board, ACT, EAB, and/or Technolutions since Jan. 1, 2023 for the creation of advertising audience segments or profiles, and;
  4.  All invoices and purchase orders between the university and College Board, ACT, EAB, and/or Technolutions since Jan. 1, 2023 for targeted digital advertising campaigns”
We have determined that the University at Albany may possess records responsive to parts 1-4 your request: however, the records you are requesting in part 1 may contain information exempt to FOIL disclosure under the Freedom of Information Law section $ 87.2 (d) (“Trade Secrets”). The process to ascertain whether an exemption to disclosure can be granted takes usually 6 to 8 weeks. Any redactions applied because of this process will be reviewed by our legal office. To allow for these processes, I am tentatively setting the records submission date to Friday, October 10th, 2025.

You may appeal this determination in part or in whole, in writing, citing any basis consistent with the Public Officers Law for release of the records within (30) days of your receipt hereof to:

FOIL Appeals Office
c/o Office of General Counsel
The State University of New York

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Student data purchases and licensing agreements
communications.communication:
Dear Requestor:

I am writing to follow up on your FOIL request dated January 27th, 2025, for the following:


  1.  All active contracts and statements of works between the university and College Board, ACT Inc., EAB Inc., and/or Technolutions (also known as Slate.org)…;
  2.  All invoices and purchase orders for high school student data purchased and/or licensed by the university since Jan. 1, 2023 from College Board, ACT, EAB, and/or Technolutions;
  3.  All invoices and purchase orders between the university and College Board, ACT, EAB, and/or Technolutions since Jan. 1, 2023 for the creation of advertising audience segments or profiles, and;
  4.  All invoices and purchase orders between the university and College Board, ACT, EAB, and/or Technolutions since Jan. 1, 2023 for targeted digital advertising campaigns”
Please be advised that due to staffing limitations, the volume of records requested, and the current format that the records are stored and maintained in, we require more time to gather records from the identified campus offices. Additionally, once we have completed our process of gathering records, we will require additional time to determine whether the records you are requesting contain information exempt from disclosure pursuant to NYS Public Officers Article 6, § 87.2 (b) {Unwarranted invasion of privacy} and /or 87.2 (d) {Trade secrets}.

The process to ascertain whether an exemption to disclosure can be granted pursuant to NYS Public Officers Law Article 6, § 89.5 (a) - (h) can take up to 8 weeks. This process involves notifying the other party in our executed contracts to inform them of their right to request redactions. Any redactions applied because of this process will be reviewed by our General Counsel. To allow for these processes, I am tentatively setting the records submission date to Tuesday, December 9th, 2025.

We appreciate your patience as we continue working on your FOIL request. We anticipate being able to produce records on a rolling basis as soon as we can.

You may appeal this determination in part or in whole, in writing, citing any basis consistent with the Public Officers Law for release of the records within (30) days of your receipt hereof to:

FOIL Appeals Office
c/o Office of General Counsel
The State University of New York

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Student data purchases and licensing agreements
communications.communication:
Dear Requestor:

I am writing about your FOIL request dated January 27th, 2025, for the following records:

Part 1: All active contracts and statements of works between the university and College Board, ACT Inc., EAB Inc., and/or Technolutions (also known as Slate.org) for the provision of the following goods or services:

  1.  The purchase, licensing, or other use of data about high school students. This includes student name list buys and subscriptions to prospective student search services.
  2.  The creation of advertising audience segments or profiles of high school students for the purpose of marketing campaigns.
  3.  The creation and/or execution of digital marketing campaigns targeting high school students.

Parts 2 – 4: All invoices and purchase orders for high school student data purchased and/or licensed by the university since Jan. 1, 2023 from College Board, ACT, EAB, and/or Technolutions, for:

  1.  Any instructions given to the vendor(s) regarding filters applied to the student data;
  2.  Creation of advertising audience segments or profiles, and;
  3.  Targeted digital advertising campaigns.

I have determined that the University may possess records responsive to your request for the following Vendors: College Board, EAB, Inc. and Technolutions (Slate). We require more time to research and review the records identified that are associated with College Board, EAB, Inc. and Technolutions (Slate), to make sure that they are responsive to parts 1-4 of your request.
Please be advised that the University at Albany does not have possession of records responsive to your request and no responsive records can be found following a diligent search for the following portions of your FOIL request: ACT Inc.


Please be advised that the records you are requesting require us to allow the other party in our executed agreement(s) to request that trade secrets be redacted pursuant to NYS Public Officers Law Article 6, § 89.5 (a) - (h). The process to ascertain whether exemptions to disclosure can be granted can take up to 8 weeks. Any redactions applied because of this process will be reviewed by our General Counsel. Please also note that there are potentially hundreds of pages of records associated with the 3 vendors identified above. Because of the volume of records that will require a thorough review for exemptions, I am tentatively extending the deadline to produce records to Thursday, June 4th, 2026. This extension considers the current amount of open FOIL requests the University has, staffing and resource limitations and the location and format the records are stored in. Thank you in advance for your patience as we continue to work on your FOIL request.

You may appeal this determination in part or in whole, in writing, citing any basis consistent with the Public Officers Law for release of the records within (30) days of your receipt hereof to:

FOIL Appeals Office
c/o Office of General Counsel
The State University of New York
H. Carl McCall SUNY Building – 353 Broadway
Albany, NY 12246
518-445-4040

Best,

Andrea Lurie Miller
Records Management and Access Officer
Office of Enterprise Risk Management & Compliance

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Student data purchases and licensing agreements
communications.communication:
Dear Requestor:

I am writing about your FOIL Request dated January 27th, 2025, for records the University possesses pertaining to College Board.

I have determined that the attached are responsive to your request. Please note the contract has been redacted as some contents were found to be exempt from disclosure under Public Officers Law Article 6, section 87(2)(g) {… because they are inter-agency or intra-agency materials that are not: i. statistical or factual tabulations or data; ii. instructions to staff that affect the public; iii. final agency policy or determinations; or iv. external audits, including but not limited to audits performed by the comptroller and the federal government.} The email correspondence between the University and the New York State Office of Attorney General are deliberative.

Additionally, the attached purchase orders have been redacted as some content were found to be exempt from disclosure under Public Officers Law Article 6, sections 87(2)(b) {Records that, if disclosed, would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy under the provisions of subdivision two of section eighty-nine of Article 6}. Account numbers and internal identifiers have been removed.

This concludes our response to your request and there is no charge for same. Thank you again for your patience.

You may appeal this determination in part or in whole, in writing, citing any basis consistent with the Public Officers Law for release of the records within (30) days of your receipt hereof to:

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (McLennan County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (McLennan County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Oregon Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting a copy of the following records, that have already been requested to Eagle Point Police Department. By requesting previously produced records, I am hopeful that this request will require minimal labor to locate, review and produce the records. by please let me know if you need any additional information or clarification regarding the records I am seeking.

The records I am seeking are:

(1) all policies Eagle Point Police Department has that oversee and/or regulate the operation and use of Automated License Plate Readers, including the operation and use of Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares
(2) a copy of any and all invoices Eagle Point Police Department has between any and all Automated License Plate Readers softwares and programs, including Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares
(3) a map of all ALPR cameras that Eagle Point Police Department owns, operates or has access to
(4) a copy of any and all license and user agreements, contracts, memorandums of understanding or other similar type documents that describe and/or regulate the use, retention, collection, and sharing of ALPR data including but not limited to the agreements between Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares

By requesting previously produced records, I am hopeful that this request will require minimal labor to locate, review and produce the records. Please let me know if you have any questions.

Please segregate all non-exempt material, and “black out” rather than “white out” any exempt material. Please cite specific exemptions in every instance that  the public body claims an exemption.

Thank you so much!

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Southern Oregon Flock Off

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Secretary of State)

communications.subject: Arizona Public Records Law Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Secretary of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arizona Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

communications.subject: [NYS OAG FOIL Request Center] General FOIL Request :: G000695-100925
communications.communication:
April 14, 2026 RE:  Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) Request Reference # G000695-100925 Dear Julia  Rock: This email is in response to the above-­referenced FOIL request. Please be advised that the Office of the Attorney General (“OAG”) has completed its diligent search and has located no additional records that respond to your request. 

Please be advised that some of the records that respond to your request were withheld in their entirety, or portions of the records previously provided to you were redacted and appear as blacked out in the documents produced to you.  These records are exempt from disclosure pursuant to: • Public Officers Law § 87(2)(b), because their disclosure would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy; • Public Officers Law § 87(2)(e), because the documents requested were compiled for law enforcement purposes and would, if disclosed, interfere with law enforcement investigations or judicial proceedings; or identify a confidential source or disclose confidential information relating to a criminal investigation. • Public Officers Law § 87(2)(f), because their disclosure could endanger someone’s life or safety; and • Public Officers Law § 87(2)(g), because the records are inter-agency or intra-agency materials. You have a right to appeal the foregoing decision.  If you elect to do so, you must appeal in writing, within 30 days, to Kathryn Sheingold, Records Appeals Officer.  Your appeal can be sent by email to FOIL.Appeals@ag.ny.gov or by mail to State of New York, Office of the Attorney General, Division of Appeals and Opinions, The Capitol, Albany, New York 12224.  You can reach the Records Appeals Officer by telephone at (518) 776-2009. 

Please note that records, or portions thereof, that respond to your request are drawn from the opinions, statements, or other content provided by a complainant and do not necessarily reflect the views of the OAG.  You should not construe any information provided to you by this Office to imply the Attorney General’s endorsement, recommendation, or disapproval of a given entity. 

Any numerical or other data provided by the OAG is compiled by sources believed to be reliable.  However, because of the dynamic nature of this Office’s receipt and processing of complaint information, we cannot guarantee that this information is perfect. 

When you review complaint information, you may wish to consider the entity’s size and its volume of business transactions.  You should also note the nature of the complaints and an entity’s responses to them, because that information may be equally, if not more, important than the number of complaints received by the OAG. 
This is our second and final response to the above-referenced request. 
Accordingly, this FOIL request is now closed. 

Sincerely, 

Records Access Officer

Specifically, I am requesting:

All addenda to the solicitation, and any questions and answers (Q&A) or clarifications issued to vendors.

title: Chief FOIA officer's recent email (Library of Congress)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Chief FOIA officer's recent email (Library of Congress)
communications.communication:
Dear Library of Congress,

On behalf of myself and Fiat Fiendum, I hereby request the following records:

1. The one email in your chief FOIA officer's email inbox or equivalent which was received nearest and before October 19, 2024, midnight Eastern time, regardless of subject matter.
2. The one email in your chief FOIA officer's email spam folder or equivalent which was received nearest and before October 19, 2024, midnight Eastern time, regardless of subject matter.
3. The one email in your chief FOIA officer's sent email folder or equivalent which was sent nearest and before October 19, 2024, midnight Eastern time, regardless of subject matter.
4. The total full-time equivalent count of people who work for your FOIA office as of October 19, 2024.

This request is expressly limited to Library of Congress's own FOIA office. Please do not refer it to any other agency or division. This request is expressly not a Privacy Act request, and no PA waiver will be provided.

Please provide the email records in EML, Maildir, MBOX, MIX, MSG, or PST format only — not PDF, Word, image, etc — including all metadata (e.g. full email headers as received/sent) and attachments, without alteration other than narrowly tailored electronic redaction of specifically claimed exemptions that do not affect any part of non exempt information (and, in particular, without rasterization).

Please provide the fourth record as a statement in your response letter, or at your option, as the most readily available record, in original electronic format as currently stored on your computer systems, which includes that information.

Please provide all records either as individual files, or as an unencrypted ZIP file, uploaded to MuckRock. Do not apply any encryption, DRM, or other restriction.

I request fee waiver, because
* Fiat Fiendum is a 501(c)(3) non-profit;
* this request is for public interest, non-commercial purposes;
* these records would give a snapshot of insight into the everyday processes of FOIA offices; and
* these records are being requested in order to conduct a government-wide audit of agencies' FOIA compliance.

In the event that you deny fee waiver, please process this request as-is, because I will not agree to any fees.

Please be sure to provide:
* 5 USC 552(a)(6)(A)(i)(II) FOIA Public Liaison information
* 5 USC 552(a)(6)(A)(i)(III)(aa) appeal information
* 5 USC 552(a)(6)(A)(i)(III)(bb) OGIS information
* 5 USC 552(a)(7)(A) tracking number
* 5 USC 552(a)(7)(B)(ii) completion ETA

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,
Sai
President, Fiat Fiendum, Inc.

P.S. Sai is my full legal name.

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Chief FOIA officer's recent email (Library of Congress)
communications.communication:

title: Nevada Public Records Request - Mountain West Tournament Location Agreement (University of Nevada, Las Vegas)

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: Nevada Public Records Request - Mountain West Tournament Location Agreement (University of Nevada, Las Vegas)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request records related to the University of Nevada, Las Vegas decision to remain in the Mountain West Conference and the agreement regarding the Mountain West men’s and women’s basketball championships being held at the Thomas & Mack Center.
I request the following records created between January 1, 2023 and December 31, 2024:
1. Emails, text messages, or other communications discussing the location of the Mountain West basketball championships, including references to “Thomas & Mack Center,” “Las Vegas host agreement,” or “conference tournament location.”
2. The documents of understanding or other agreements between UNLV and the Mountain West Conference related to conference membership through 2032.
3. Documents, reports, or presentations related to financial incentives or guarantees associated with hosting the Mountain West basketball championships in Las Vegas.
4. Communications between UNLV leadership and Mountain West Conference officials regarding tournament hosting arrangements.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Maia Ancho

title: ALPR Data Sharing, Vendor Contracts, and Query Logs (Paso Robles Police Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. The current written ALPR Privacy and Usage Policy as required by Vehicle Code §2413(b), including all versions in effect since January 1, 2022.
2. All contracts, MOUs, data-sharing agreements, or other agreements with any ALPR vendor, including Flock Safety and Vigilant Solutions/Motorola Solutions, since January 1, 2022.
3. All contracts, MOUs, or data-sharing agreements with any federal agency — including ICE, CBP/Border Patrol, HSI, DEA, ATF, FBI, or any U.S. military branch — or any out-of-state law enforcement agency, related to ALPR data access.
4. A complete list of all agencies or entities authorized to query the SLO Sheriff's ALPR database, or that have been authorized to do so since January 1, 2022.
5. All audit logs from the date the SLO County Sheriff's Office first activated any ALPR system or vendor platform (including Flock Safety and Vigilant Solutions/Motorola Solutions) through the date of your response to this request, showing all ALPR database queries, including requesting agency name, date/time, and any available purpose or case number fields. Personal vehicle identifying data may be redacted. If providing the full contract period is deemed burdensome, please provide logs from January 1, 2024, through the date of response as a minimum, and specify the full date range of available records.
6. All notifications received from ALPR vendors — including Flock Safety — about unauthorized access, configuration errors, data breaches, or changes to any National Lookup, Statewide Lookup, or similar settings.
7. All notifications, security alerts, or vendor communications related to password theft, account compromises, or unauthorized system access.
8. Any internal communications since January 1, 2022, regarding the Sheriff's ALPR program's compliance with California privacy law or the Attorney General's enforcement actions against other agencies.
9. Any correspondence between the SLO County Sheriff's Office and the California Attorney General's Office regarding ALPR compliance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Debra Appelbaum

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Flock Accounts
communications.communication:
To the FOIA Officer of Lincolnwood Police Department,

This request is made under the provisions of Illinois Freedom of Information Act. for the following records:

Records identifying all individuals who have had user access to the department’s Flock Safety system.

This request covers the entire period from the department’s initial adoption of Flock through the date this request is processed.

Please include all available information for each user, including:
• First and last name
• Email address
• Phone number
• Job title or position
• Role or permissions within the system
• Identity provider
• Date of last login
• Whether access was enabled or disabled for functions such as User Management, Search, Device Management, Hotlist Tool, etc.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the case of any applicable fees, it is requested that an estimate of the total charges be provided prior to fulfilling this request. The preferred method of receipt is through electronic delivery.

Your assistance and cooperation in this matter are greatly appreciated. A response within 5 business days, as the statute requires, is anticipated.

With thanks,

David S. (he/him/his), Lucy Parsons Labs

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Flock Accounts
communications.communication:

> Mr. ████████████:

However, Mr. Rogina forwarded me your correspondence indicating that a report could be generated. This was extremely helpful.  The Village went back and was able to produce a report. title: Attn: Ohio Open Records Law request about Flock Safety (Jackson Township Police Department Stark County)

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Attn: Ohio Open Records Law request about Flock Safety (Jackson Township Police Department Stark County)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

All records referencing the department’s relationship with the company Flock Safety to install automated license-plate readers (ALPRs), including but not limited to the following:

- Flock transparency portals and reports
- Flock services agreement(s)
- Purchase order(s)
- Flock network audits
- Search audits
- Flock logs
- Contract(s) between the department and Flock
- Flock sales pitches and recruitment materials
- Meeting minutes
- Training materials, including but not limited to presentations, powerpoint slides, and videos
- Department regulations referencing the Flock system and/or license plate readers
- Any and all communications, including but not limited to emails, email attachments, text messages, voice mail messages, between agency officials and Flock employees

When making this request please make sure to search the following key terms:

- “@flocksafety.com” 

When making this request please exclude newsletters, automated notifications, Google alerts, or other mass-distributed messages.

I would like these records in electronic format, transmitted via email or other digital method; please refrain from sending paper copies of the records. If you anticipate that providing me with all responsive records will take longer than two weeks, I ask that you provide records to me on a rolling basis as they become available. In any event, do not destroy any records that may potentially contain responsive documents.
 
If you determine that any of the information I’ve requested qualifies for an exemption from disclosure, I ask you to note whether the exemption is discretionary, and if so whether it is necessary in this case to exercise your discretion to withhold the information. In any event, please provide a signed notification citing the legal authorities on which you rely if you determine that any of the information is exempt and will not be disclosed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Frank Miciano

communications.communication:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

All agendas, minutes, and other materials associated with the meetings of the Citizen Advisory Committee (CAC).  I would like these records to be obtained from 1/1/2023 to present.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael

I respectfully request access to and copies of  Verra Mobility's RFP records related to the procurement of a school bus stop-arm camera associated with Austin ISD RFP submitted on November 15, 2024 .  

Solicitation/Bid: RFP# 25RFP029

Issuing Entity: Austin Independent School District

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Jon Smith

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Updated Investigative Memo Database
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

In FOIA release #N017813-062525, Investigative Memo 5620 states, "The R/I checked the Investigations Unit case database and found no similar complaint having previously been made to this office."

Records sufficient to show the following:

1. From the Investigations Unit case database, a report with the investigative file number, date, type of incident, school and network from 2010-current.

Please supply the file in Excel format.

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Miriam Bhimani

Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center. 

Track the issue status and respond at: https://cps.mycusthelp.com/WEBAPP//_rs/RequestEdit.aspx?rid=19199

RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. Please note that school and networks are not fields used in reports generated in the normal course of business. These portions of your request would require the creation of records that were not previously maintained or prepared in the normal course of business and that doing so is not required by the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140/1). 
You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: Leah Bartelt; Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 E-mail: public.access@ilag.gov 877-299-3642 You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. Sincerely, Patricia Majchrowicz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

title: Cabell County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Cabell County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Drew Solt 
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
Mar. 12, 2026

To whom it may concern,

Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Cabell County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting:
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through today
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 to today
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 to today
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds

If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes.

The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records.

If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.
Thank you for considering my request.

Sincerely,  Drew Solt 
740-243-6793
drewsolt@yahoo.com
