communications.communication:
________________________________
From: Beth Zerkle <bzerkle@cabellcounty.org>
Sent: Monday, March 30, 2026 12:18 PM
To: drewsolt@yahoo.com <drewsolt@yahoo.com>
Subject: March 13, 2026 FOIA Request


Good afternoon,



In response to your FOIA request dated March 13, 2026, “The custodian of any public records, unless otherwise expressly provided by statute, shall furnish proper and reasonable opportunities for inspection and examination of the records in his or her office and reasonable facilities for making memoranda or abstracts therefrom, during the usual business hours, to all persons having occasion to make examination of them.”  W. Va. Code § 29B-1-3(c) (emphasis supplied).



The Cabell County Commission office has received your FOIA request regarding opioid funds, which was sent to the Cabell County Assessor’s Office.  Below is a link to the website with the requested information:



https://cabellcounty.org/government/opioid_settlement_applications.php



Further, materials are available in the Commission office, where you may inspect and copy them (WV Code §29B-1-3) during regular business hours (8:30 am – 4:30 pm Monday – Friday) with five (5) business days' written notice.



Thank  you,

Beth Zerkle

County Administrator

Cabell County Commission

750 5th Avenue, Suite 309

Huntington, WV 25701

O 304-526-8634

M 681-294-5026

title: FOIL Request – Flock Safety Surveillance Cameras – Pine Plains

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: FOIL Request – Flock Safety Surveillance Cameras – Pine Plains
communications.communication:
Dear Records Custodian,

Under the provisions of the New York Freedom of Information Law (FOIL), N.Y. Public Officers Law §§ 84–90, I hereby request access to and copies of records held by the Town of Pine Plains, Dutchess County, relating to the planning, proposal, procurement, authorization, or attempted deployment of Flock Safety surveillance cameras or automated license plate reader (ALPR) systems.

This request is made in part due to reporting concerning the Pine Plains surveillance camera proposal:

https://www.timesunion.com/hudsonvalley/news/article/pine-plains-flock-surveillance-cameras-outcry-21958221.php

The purpose of this request is to determine the origin, authorization process, procurement activity, proposed locations, and communications surrounding the surveillance camera system, including identifying which individual, agency, or vendor first initiated discussions or planning for the program.

Timeframe: January 1, 2023 – Present, including any earlier records referencing or relating to Flock Safety, automated license plate readers, or surveillance cameras within the Town of Pine Plains.

Records Requested

For the purpose of this request, the records I seek include, but are not limited to the following:

1. Contracts and Agreements

All contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, purchase orders, vendor agreements, procurement documents, or proposals involving the Town of Pine Plains and Flock Safety or any related entities concerning surveillance cameras, automated license plate readers, or related monitoring technology.

This includes draft contracts, executed agreements, amendments, pilot program agreements, trial program documentation, cancellation notices, procurement justifications, and contract termination records.

Legal Basis: Contracts involving public funds or public infrastructure are public records subject to disclosure under N.Y. Public Officers Law § 87.

2. Records Identifying Project Initiation

All records reflecting the initial introduction, proposal, solicitation, or outreach regarding Flock Safety cameras or ALPR systems, including records identifying:

• the individual or office that first proposed or recommended the system  
• the first communications between the Town and Flock Safety  
• vendor outreach to town officials  
• internal discussions about introducing the technology  
• marketing or sales outreach from vendors  

3. Internal Communications

All internal communications including emails, memoranda, text messages, chat messages, letters, meeting notes, or other correspondence among:

• the Town Supervisor  
• Town Board members  
• Pine Plains Police Department personnel  
• Town administrative staff  
• Town legal counsel  
• Highway or Building Department personnel  
• other municipal offices  

that discuss:

• Flock Safety  
• automated license plate readers  
• surveillance cameras  
• camera deployments  
• vendor procurement or negotiations  
• surveillance technology adoption  

4. Communications With External Entities

All communications between the Town of Pine Plains and:

• representatives of Flock Safety  
• technology vendors or consultants  
• Dutchess County officials  
• local, state, or federal law enforcement agencies  
• regional intelligence or real-time crime centers  

concerning surveillance cameras, ALPR systems, or the Flock Safety program.

5. Vendor Demonstrations and Sales Outreach

All records relating to vendor presentations, product demonstrations, marketing materials, or sales outreach regarding Flock Safety or related surveillance technology, including:

• presentation decks  
• vendor proposals  
• demonstration scheduling emails  
• meeting invitations or call summaries  
• webinar invitations  
• follow-up communications  

6. Proposed Camera Locations and Site Selection

All records identifying, evaluating, or discussing proposed or potential locations for surveillance cameras or automated license plate reader systems within the Town of Pine Plains.

This includes but is not limited to:

• maps showing proposed camera placements  
• GIS maps or infrastructure maps  
• deployment plans  
• proposed installation locations or intersections  
• roadway monitoring points  
• entry or exit routes to the town  
• communications discussing site selection  
• vendor recommendations for placement  
• field surveys or site inspections  
• infrastructure or engineering assessments related to camera placement  

7. Deployment and Planning Documents

All planning or operational records relating to the surveillance system, including:

• deployment plans  
• camera location maps  
• infrastructure assessments  
• equipment specifications  
• installation schedules  
• operational capability descriptions  
• technology architecture documents  

8. Financial and Procurement Records

All records relating to funding, budgeting, or procurement of the surveillance system, including:

• invoices  
• payment records  
• purchase authorizations  
• budget allocations  
• grant applications  
• cost analyses  
• procurement justifications  
• financial approvals  

9. Meeting Records

All agendas, minutes, recordings, transcripts, or notes from Town Board meetings, committee meetings, departmental meetings, or internal meetings where the Flock Safety system or surveillance camera program was discussed, proposed, approved, or canceled.

10. Oversight, Audit, and Compliance Records

Any audit reports, legal opinions, compliance reviews, investigations, or internal analyses relating to the surveillance camera program or procurement process.

11. Data Access, Sharing, and Integration

All records relating to data access or sharing involving the surveillance system, including:

• agreements allowing other agencies to access camera data  
• policies governing license plate reader searches  
• records concerning integration with regional surveillance networks  
• data retention policies  
• privacy or civil liberties assessments  

Electronic Records and Metadata

For all responsive electronic records, including emails and digital documents, I request that records be produced in their native electronic format where possible, including all available metadata and email header information.

This includes, but is not limited to:

• sender and recipient information  
• CC and BCC fields  
• full email header routing data  
• timestamps for sending and receipt  
• message ID fields  
• attachment metadata  
• document creation dates  
• last modified dates  
• author fields  
• file path information where available  

Providing metadata is necessary to determine the origin, timing, and authorship of communications relating to the surveillance camera proposal.

Request for Segregable Non-Exempt Portions

Should any portion of the requested records be deemed exempt from disclosure, I request the release of any reasonably segregable non-exempt portions in accordance with N.Y. Public Officers Law § 87(2).

Preservation of Records

Please note your obligation not to destroy or alter any records potentially responsive to this request. Once a FOIL request has been received, agencies are required to preserve all responsive records during processing.

Fee Waiver Request

I request a fee waiver for the processing of this request as the information sought is in the public interest and its disclosure will contribute significantly to public understanding of government surveillance programs and decision-making processes.

If potential fees exceed $200, please inform me before proceeding.

Public Availability of Communications

Please be advised that communications relating to this request, including incoming and outgoing correspondence, may be publicly published on transparency platforms such as MuckRock.com.

Statutory Response Time

As required under N.Y. Public Officers Law § 89(3), I look forward to acknowledgment of this request within five business days.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

Jordan Lassiter  
Independent Investigative Journalist

Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center. 

Track the issue status and respond at: https://cps.mycusthelp.com/WEBAPP//_rs/RequestEdit.aspx?rid=19223

communications.subject: Non-Commercial FOIA Request :: N019223-031126
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
03/26/2026 Miriam Bhimani 
Dear Miriam Bhimani: RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019223-031126 Thank you for using the Chicago Public Schools FOIA Center. You requested the following: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

1. A log of all emails to/from the Fae Rabin, Chief of Staff to the Board of Education, from January 1, 2026 to January 31, 2026. 

Please search all CPS email accounts as well as personal accounts used for public business. The log should include but not be limited to: sender, recipient(s), date and time, subject line, and attachment. 

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Miriam Bhimani 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2238489/ RESPONSE: The CPS FOIA Office has reviewed your request and determined that is unduly burdensome to fulfill as currently formulated. The search parameters you provided yielded an initial count of approximately 3,223 lines of emails and attachments. 
In order to comply with this request,  each and every of the approximate 3,223 lines in this log would have to be reviewed to determine if it is exempt from disclosure. The processing and reviewing such a large volume of records for responsiveness as well as for information exempt from release under the Illinois Freedom of Information Act,  such as personally private information, preliminary drafts of records and information exempt from disclosure under the Illinois School Student Records Act (ISSRA) would be estimated to take dozens of hours. This would clearly burden the operations of CPS beyond the public interest in this information. 
This is not an automatic denial of your request. Pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(g), the District is extending an opportunity to confer to narrow your request in writing, to more manageable proportions within five (5) business days. If you choose not to do so, this response will be considered denied pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(g). You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: Leah Bartelt; Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 E-mail: public.access@ilag.gov 877-299-3642 You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. Sincerely, Patricia Majchrowicz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2238489/ Narrowed: Please narrow this request to January 1, 2026 - January 10, 2026 RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. Please note that certain private information was redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7. Specifically, section 7(1)(b) which exempts certain private information as defined by section 2(c-5) and section 7(1)(c) which exempts personal information contained within public records, the disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, unless the disclosure is consented to in writing by the individual subject of the information. “Unwarranted invasion of personal privacy” means the disclosure of information that is highly personal or objectionable to a reasonable person and in which the subject’s right to privacy outweighs any legitimate public interest in obtaining the information. Student information has been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7.5 (r) which exempts from inspection or copying information prohibited from being disclosed by the Illinois School Student Records Act (ISSRA). ISSRA generally prohibits the disclosure of “school student records” and defines a “school student record” as “any writing or other recorded information concerning a student and by which a student may be individually identified, maintained by a school or at is direction or by an employee of a school, regardless of how or where the information is stored.” (105 ILCS 10/2(d). Section 7.5(h) exempts from disclosure “information the disclosure of which is exempted under the State Officials and Employees Ethics Act, and records of any lawfully created State or local inspector general’s office that would be exempt if created or obtained by an Executive Inspector General’s office.” 5 ILCS 140/7.5(h). You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: Leah Bartelt; Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 E-mail: public.access@ilag.gov 877-299-3642 You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. Sincerely, Patricia Majchrowicz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

title: Federal government Rule 11 letters (Department of Justice, Civil Rights Division)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Federal government Rule 11 letters (Department of Justice, Civil Rights Division)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. In any litigation before a federal district court on behalf of the United States or any agency, department, official, or employee thereof, any letter sent by your attorneys containing any of the following terms: 
a. "Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 11"
b. "Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, Rule 11"
c. "FRCP 11"
d. "Fed. R. Civ. P. 11"
e. "Fed. R. Civ. P., Rule 11"

2. Any policies, manuals, or guidance (not including guidance specific to a particular case) concerning pursuit of Rule 11 sanctions.

This request is limited to correspondence sent (or policies, manuals, or guidance utilized) on or after January 1, 2020.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Adam Steinbaugh

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] Freedom of Information Act Request: Federal government Rule 11 letters (Department of Justice, Civil Rights Division)
communications.communication:
Mr. Steinbaugh,


Thank you for contacting the Civil Rights Division Freedom of Information Act Unit. The Civil Rights Division is in receipt of your below FOIA request for any litigation before a federal district court on behalf of the United States or any agency, department, official, or employee thereof, any letter sent by your attorneys containing any of the following terms: "Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 11"; "Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, Rule 11"; "FRCP 11"; "Fed. R. Civ. P. 11"; "Fed. R. Civ. P., Rule 11".
The number of potential custodians is too expansive to conduct a search of approximately 700 career employees.  Therefore, if you want federal records from the Civil Rights Division, please narrow your request to target specific custodians or Section within the Civil Rights Division you would like a search to be conducted (i.e., political appointees, AAG, Disability Rights, Criminal, Special Litigation, Employment Litigation, Housing and Civil Enforcement).

Upon receipt of this additional information, we will search our files for information responsive to your request

Thank You,

FOI/PA Unit
U.S. Department of Justice
Civil Rights Division

Date Received: December 1, 2026

FOI/PA No. CASENUMBER          26-00043-F


Dear Mr. Steinbaugh:

The Civil Rights Division acknowledges receipt of your FOIA request, which was received in this office on the date indicated above.  Your request seeks access to records about cases opened since January 1, 2025 through November 1, 2025, where the case description or subject matter involves the First Amendment.  Please read this letter carefully because it contains important information about your request.

            We have initiated a search to locate records, if any, that are within the scope of your request.  We will follow up with you once searches have been completed and any responsive records subject to FOIA/Privacy Act have been processed to segregate non-exempt information for release.

            As a result of the large number of FOIA and Privacy Acts requests received by the Civil Rights Division, we may encounter delays in processing your request.  To ensure that requesters are treated fairly, the Division processes requests in the approximate order of receipt.  Please be assured that your request is being handled as equitably and promptly as possible.  As a result of the large number of FOIA and Privacy Acts requests received by the Civil Rights Division, we may encounter delays in processing your request.  To ensure that requesters are treated fairly, the Division processes requests in the approximate order of receipt.  Please be assured that your request is being handled as equitably and promptly as possible. If you have any questions regarding the status of your request, you may contact Andrea Banks at crt.foiacomms@usdoj.gov<mailto:crt.foiacomms@usdoj.gov>. Please use the subject line: FOIA Request 26-00043-F (ADB) and reference the FOIA/PA No. above in any communications with the Civil Rights Division about your request


Additionally, you may contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) at the National Archives and Records Administration to inquire about the FOIA mediation services they offer.  The contact information for OGIS is:  Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001; e-mail at ogis@nara.gov<mailto:ogis@nara.gov>; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448; or facsimile at 202-741-5769.  Please reference the FOIA/PA No. above in any correspondence with OGIS.

Finally, if you are not satisfied with the Civil Rights Division's determination in response to this request, you may administratively appeal by writing to the Director, Office of Information Policy (OIP), United States Department of Justice, 441 G Street, NW, 6th Floor, Washington, D.C. 20530, or you may submit an appeal through OIP's FOIA STAR portal by creating an account following the instructions on OIP's website: https://www.justice.gov/oip/submit-and-track-request-or-appeal. Your appeal must be postmarked or electronically transmitted within 90 days of the date of my response to your request. If you submit your appeal by mail, both the letter and the envelope should be clearly marked "Freedom of Information Act Appeal."  Please reference the FOIA/PA No. above in any correspondence with OIP.

Thank you for your interest in the Civil Rights Division.


Andrea D. Banks

950 Pennsylvania Ave. N.W.
4CON 6th Floor

title: ICE Detainers and 287(g) Policies (Polk County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: ICE Detainers and 287(g) Policies (Polk County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Data from January 1, 2023 to the present showing:
1. The number of ICE detainer requests received
2. The number of detainers honored
3. The number of detainers declined
4. The number of individuals transferred to ICE custody
5. Current written policies, procedures, or guidance related to:
-ICE detainers
-Notification to ICE of release dates
-Participation in the 287(g) program (if applicable)
6. Emails sent or received by the Sheriff, Undersheriff, or Chief Deputies between January 1, 2023 and the present containing the term:
“287(g)”

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Jasmine Lewin

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: 988 hotline removal documents
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Guidance documents, memos and any other department records pertaining to the end of the 988 National Suicide and Crisis Lifeline's LGBTQ+ youth and young adult services, also known as the “Press 3 option", set to cease on July 17. In these documents, the services may be referred to as "LGB+ youth services".

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, processed by a representative of the news media/press and is made in the process of news gathering and not for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ileana Garnand

communications.subject: SAMHSA FOIA Request Acknowledgement: Control Number 06262025S247
communications.communication:
Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration 
 
SAMHSA FOIA Service Center 
 
Dear Ileana Garnand: 
 
Thank you for contacting the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration's (SAMHSA) Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) Service Center. This letter acknowledges receipt of your FOIA request dated 06/26/2025. Your request has been assigned request number 06262025S247. Please refer to this number on any future correspondence. 
 
Processing times will depend on the complexity of your request and whether sensitive records, voluminous records, extensive searches, and/or consultation with other U.S. Department of Health and Human Services components or other executive branch agencies are involved. Pursuant to Departmental regulations, 28 CFR Part 16.10, processing fees may be charged. (https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-28/chapter-I/part-16/subpart-A/section-16.10) 
 
Contact the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration's FOIA Officer at foia@samhsa.gov, if you have any questions or are not satisfied with any aspect of the processing and handling of your request. (mailto:foia@samhsa.gov) 
 
Sincerely, 
 
The SAMHSA FOIA Service Center

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The case file for case ID# 9A-KC-3112208. This case was mentioned in the files for another case, ID# 9A-LV-4729965, which the FBI released today.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an assistant editor at Reason Magazine who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: Flock Email Metadata
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Metadata for all email communications which included anyone with a Flock Safety email (*@flocksafety.com) as either the sender and/or receiver, including being CCed or BCCed, between 11/25/25 and 03/10/26.

The specific metadata data points I'm interested in are: to address(es), from address, cc address(es), bcc address(es), sent date/time, received date/time, subject line, and names of all attachments. This request does not seek the contents of the emails themselves, and only the specified email metadata. Please do not include duplicate emails. Please provide me with the records in an Excel format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael

The City Clerk’s office is in receipt of your request.

Please note this request may take more than 72 hours to complete. You will be notified when it’s completed.
Be advised that the City of Joplin passed Resolution No. 2004-038 on the 16th day of August 2004 which pertains to charging a fee for obtaining information.  This resolution allows the City to charge for staff research time and for copying fees.

If requested, we will provide you with an estimate of charges before processing.  We do not waive fees for requests.

Holly Nagy

Good afternoon,

A deposit of $942.80 is required to compile your request. If the cost exceeds $942.80 you will be required to make additional payment, if actual costs are less than the estimate, a refund will be processed.

Crime Analysist 40 hours @ $23.57/hr = $942.80 – Researching every hotlist alert item listed in email subject line to check investigative status.

You can reduce the amount of the deposit by allowing the removal of the subject lines pertaining to hotlist items.  That would result in only the IT Network Admin time 1 hour @ $38.72/hr.

Please let us know which option you choose and upon receipt of payment, we will begin processing your request.

Payment can be mailed to 602 S. Main Street, Joplin, MO 64801.  Please make payment out to City of Joplin and mark the envelope attn City Clerk.

Currently, we do not accept payments by phone or online. You may also drop off your payment at the Finance Department. Take your payment to the third floor and let them know you are paying for an open records request.   We are open Monday-Friday 8AM-5PM.

Holly Nagy

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #2026-USMS-000010
communications.communication: I hereby appeal the adequacy of the search and all withholdings. This appeal includes the U.S. Marshals' response. Specific challenges to search adequacy include but are not limited to: 1) the timeframe of the search; 2) the files searched; 3) the custodians searched; 4) the terms searched; 5) the failure to conduct an iterative search based on the initial findings. Specific challenges to withholdings include but are not limited to: 1) the redaction of the email domain names; 2) the redaction of names of recipients and senders of the emails; 3) the redaction of factual information not subject to exemptions 6, 7(d) or 7(e); 4) the redaction of all information for which no plausible foreseeable harm would result if the information were released.

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: ALPR audit
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Concerning Flock Safety, these files;

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

Typically the output for these audits is available in monthly batches.

3. A list of network share settings that include:
3a. Flock networks shared with me
3b. Networks that I’m sharing

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Traci Phelps

communications.subject: Re: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: ALPR audit
communications.communication:
Hello,
This correspondence is in response to your Sunshine Law request, reference Sunshine #70 TRACI PHELPS.
After researching the request, the estimated amount of time required to fulfill this request is approximately 4 hours. Pursuant to Section 610.026, RSMo, the cost for staff time is $21.00 per hour, for a total estimated cost of $84.00.
Section 610.026, RSMo, authorizes public governmental bodies to charge for the actual cost of document research, duplication, and staff time required to fulfill a Sunshine Law request.
Please be advised that any records released will be reviewed and may require redaction of information exempt from disclosure under Missouri law, including but not limited to information related to active investigations, confidential records, or other sensitive information as permitted by law.
If you wish to proceed with this request, please remit payment in the amount of $84.00 to our office and reference Sunshine #70 TRACI PHELPS. Upon receipt of payment, processing of your request will begin.
If you choose not to proceed, no further action will be taken. Should you have any questions regarding this estimate, please feel free to contact our office.
Thank you,

title: Use of force data collection (2017-2025) cities 50-100k (Milpitas Police Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Use of force data collection (2017-2025) cities 50-100k (Milpitas Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident location, area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: [External Message Added] City of Milpitas, CA public records request #26-82
communications.communication:
City of Milpitas, CA ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-82: On behalf of the City of Milpitas (“City”), this is in response to your request for City records, (“Request”) pursuant to the California Public Records Act (“PRA”) (Gov. Code, § 7920.000, et seq.). The request is listed below for your reference. Government Code section 7922.535 contemplates a local dispatch of a determination no later than ten calendar days from receipt of a public records request. As such, the City is dispatching a determination to you PRA request today. Complaints to and the investigative file of a law enforcement entity are generally exempt from disclosure pursuant to Gov. Code, § 7923.600. While certain information can be released from such reports, that information is not of the kind requested. Similarly, while certain information/records related to critical incidents as defined can be released (Gov. Code, § 7923.625; Pen. Code, § 832.7(b)(1)), the City understands that this Request is explicitly not limited to or seeking this information/records reported under such state or federal programs. Personnel records are exempt from disclosure pursuant to Gov. Code, § 7920.520. Privileged records or records otherwise exempted or prohibited from disclosure pursuant to federal or state law are also exempt pursuant to Gov. Code, § 7927.705. Peace officer personnel records are both confidential and privileged under Pen. Code, §§ 832.7 et seq., Evid. Code, §§ 1043 et seq., and Cal. Const. Art. 1, § 1. Files whose disclosure would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy are also exempt from disclosure. (Gov. Code, § 7927.700.) The PRA also exempts from disclosure “[r]ecords, the disclosure of which is exempted or prohibited pursuant to federal or state law.”( Gov. Code, § 7927.705.) Any records produced will be redacted to protect individual privacy and to prevent disclosure of information prohibited by law. The PRA applies to records only and does not obligate the City to create a record or compile information from records. (See Gov. Code, § 7922.535.)  To the extent the City maintains a non-exempt record containing the type of information being requested, the City intends to begin making appropriately redacted records available on March 16, 2026. If the City is able to make records available before this date, it will do so. Similarly, if the City requires additional time, it will contact you in writing. Suzanne Guzzetta, City Clerk, or her designee, is responsible for making exemption determinations, in consultation with the City Attorney. Please feel free to contact her should you have any questions or concerns regarding this request. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at City of Milpitas, CA.

Technical support: See our help page

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Marin Sheriff 2025 RIPA
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) Any and all records reflecting data collected and recorded by the Marin County Sheriff's Office in accordance with the California Racial and Identity Profiling Act (RIPA) from January 1 through December 31, 2025. Please provide the raw data in excel or csv format.

2) Any and all reports, records, and analyses created by, for, or at the direction of, the County of Marin that pertain to any RIPA data that has been collected by the Marin County Sheriff's Office from January 1 through  December 31, 2025 .

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tammy Edmonson

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: LACRIS Member List 2024
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am seeking a list or other documents naming the law enforcement agencies that can access the Los Angeles County Regional Identification System (LACRIS) and that can use the LACRIS facial recognition bio-metric database.  You previously provided a version of this document to Paul Tepper in 2024. I am seeking an updated version.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dave Maass

communications.subject: Public Records Request LASD-26-03112454 Acknowledgement. TRACK:00059373
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Maass

PUBLIC RECORDS ACT REQUEST LASD-26-03112454

This message is in response to your request for records under the California Public Records Act (CPRA) received by the Los Angeles County Sheriff's Department on March 10, 2026 . Your request has been assigned Request ID: LASD-26-03112454 . Please reference this number when communicating with us about your request. In your request you are seeking the following:

I AM SEEKING A LIST OR OTHER DOCUMENTS NAMING THE LAW ENFORCEMENT AGENCIES THAT CAN ACCESS THE LOS ANGELES COUNTY REGIONAL IDENTIFICATION SYSTEM (LACRIS) AND THAT CAN USE THE LACRIS FACIAL RECOGNITION BIO-METRIC DATABASE. YOU PREVIOUSLY PROVIDED A VERSION OF THE DOCUMENT TO PAUL TEPPER IN 2024. I AM SEEKING AN UPDATED VERSION.

The LASD is obligated to respond within ten (10) days of receipt* of the request as defined in Government Code section 7922.535(a) <https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displaySection.xhtml?lawCode=GOV&sectionNum=7922.535> . This time limit is subject to an extension of up to fourteen (14) additional days, which the LASD is asserting, under the circumstances defined in Government Code section 7922.535(b) <https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displaySection.xhtml?lawCode=GOV&sectionNum=7922.535> and section 7922.535(c) <https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displaySection.xhtml?lawCode=GOV&sectionNum=7922.535> .

We anticipate being able to notify you on or before April 03, 2026 if the Department has any responsive records to your request and, if applicable, provide an estimated date when the responsive records will be available.

Please note that we may redact or withhold records if there are any exempt matters impacting the privacy rights of individuals (Cal. Const., art. I, sec. 1; Gov. Code §§ 7920.000 and 7922.000 <https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displayexpandedbranch.xhtml?tocCode=GOV&division=10.&title=1.&part=&chapter=&article=> ). Other exempt matters include those protected by the attorney-client, official information, and deliberative process privileges, pending litigation exemption, personnel files exemption, matters otherwise protected from disclosure by law, or where the particular facts and circumstances warrant nondisclosure of the information.

If you have any questions, please contact the Risk Management Bureau PRA Unit Team at 323-890-5050. You may also reply to this email to either provide additional information or withdraw your request. Note: When responding to this email do not remove the tracking number in the subject line or we may not receive your message.

Sincerely,

ROBERT G. LUNA, SHERIFF

[Captain Signature]

Tri T. Hoang, Captain

Risk Management Bureau

* This date may differ from the date of your request as requests received after-hours, on weekends or holidays are entered the next business day.

MSG: UPDT: MAN:130B

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request #491
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

I write to follow up on your request below as follows:

In your first and third requests you seek a spreadsheet or report that does not exist and that cannot be created by our systems as currently configured.  I will note that all of the underlying documents are categorically exempt from the Public Records Act.  See Government Code §§ 7923.600-7923.625.  I will further note that even if we could run the kind of report you are seeking, it would arguably violate Penal Code Section 13300.  However, we were able to run a summary report with a small amount of the information you sought and it is attached.  There are no other non-exempt public records responsive to these requests.

In your second and fourth requests you seek the same categorically exempt documents you request to be summarized in your first and third requests.  For the same reasons, there are no non-exempt public records responsive to these requests.

Your fifth and sixth requests do seek public records.  Please find attached the records that we have located that are responsive.  We have redacted a small amount of personal information.
Regards,
Jen



[cid:image001.jpg@01DCCB47.84FBE710]
Jennifer Prado
Criminal Records Manager
San Mateo County Sheriff’s Office
Criminal Records Bureau
330 Bradford Street
Redwood City, CA 94063

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the most recent performance evaluation(s) for the current city/town manager and administrator. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.subject: FOIA Request :: N019343-033026
communications.communication:
Dear Ivan: 
 
Thank you for your interest in Chicago Public Schools. Your FOIA request has been submitted. Your FOIA Center reference number for tracking purposes is: N019343-033026 
You have requested the following records: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

- All completed 9-26 Pre-Election Challenge Petitions for the 2026-2028 LSC Elections 
- All completed 7-26 Withdrawal OF LSC Candidacy Forms for the 2026-2028 LSC Elections 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Ivan 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2252078/ 
 
Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

communications.subject: Non-Commercial FOIA Request :: N019343-033026
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
04/13/2026 Ivan Ivan 
Dear Ivan Ivan: RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019343-033026 RESPONSE: The CPS FOIA Office has reviewed your request and has determined the following: 
- All completed 9-26 Pre-Election Challenge Petitions for the 2026-2028 LSC Elections 
RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. 
Please note that certain private information was redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7. Specifically, section 7(1)(b) which exempts certain private information as defined by section 2(c-5) and section 7(1)(c) which exempts personal information contained within public records, the disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, unless the disclosure is consented to in writing by the individual subject of the information. “Unwarranted invasion of personal privacy” means the disclosure of information that is highly personal or objectionable to a reasonable person and in which the subject’s right to privacy outweighs any legitimate public interest in obtaining the information. - All completed 7-26 Withdrawal OF LSC Candidacy Forms for the 2026-2028 LSC Elections 
RESPONSE: The CPS FOIA Office has reviewed this portion of your request and determined that the forms you are seeking are not comprehensively tracked in any one record or database. To respond to this request the District would be required to perform a search at each of the District’s over 500 District run schools and multiple District departments, which would unduly burden the operations of the CPS and beyond the public interest. You may narrow your request in writing, by identifying certain individual or school names that you wish to obtain forms for. 
However, we are providing you with an available list of candidates that have withdrawn from the 2026-2028 LSC elections. The list is available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: Leah Bartelt; Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 E-mail: public.access@ilag.gov 877-299-3642 You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. Sincerely, Patricia Majchrowicz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

communications.subject: Sheriff Public Records Request :: S026818-010526
communications.communication:
Dear Rose Terse: 
Thank you for your interest in public records of Spokane County. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. Your request was received in this office on 1/5/2026 and given the reference number S026818-010526 for tracking purposes. Spokane County Sheriff provides access to public records in compliance with applicable laws and statutes of the State of Washington. In accordance with the public records law, the Sheriff's office has five business days to respond to a request for public records. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2169967/ 
Not all public documents are available in electronic format. If the document(s) requested are not available electronically, we will make them available for inspection or by paper copy in accordance with the Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW, does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. 
 
Please be advised that Spokane Regional Emergency Communications ("SREC") is the custodian of 911 and Crime Check audio recordings as well as audio recordings of dispatch and radio traffic. If you are attempting to obtain audio recordings of 911/Crime Check calls and/or radio traffic, you need to submit a separate request to SREC directly. You can submit a records request to SREC via their website, https://www.srec911.com. 
Spokane County

communications.subject: [Records Center] Sheriff Public Records Request :: S026818-010526
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

Rose Terse: 
In accordance with the Public Records Act, RCW 42.56.520, this message acknowledges receipt of your public records request, received by Spokane County on 1/5/2026. This request has been assigned reference code records S026818-010526. Please include this code in any communications concerning this request. 
Specifically, you requested: “To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2169967/” Our procedure is to locate records, review them for exemptions, and then prepare them for your inspection or copying. Due to the potential volume of records that may be responsive to this request, as well as the complexity of identifying and gathering the records, we anticipate processing this request in installments. We are currently processing over 1300 active requests. This does mean that it can take time to process requests, but we work to provide requestors with installments of their records on a timely basis. 
The first installment of responsive records are now available for download in the “My Request Center” of the public records portal. Instructions for downloading the files follows:  
  * Navigate to the Spokane County Public Records Center by following the blue "Public Records Center" hyperlink contained in the footer of this email. 
  * Log in with your user name and password. You will be taken directly into this request. 
  * Click on the "Download All" button directly above the blue "Files" box. *If you have a pop-up blocker, you will need to allow pop-ups for this site in order for the files to download. After they have downloaded, if the files do not open automatically, they will appear in your computer's "Downloads" folder. The attached is an unofficial list. While we have the IA investigation information and letter from the Prosecutor's office, the Sheriff's office does not make the determination of who or why someone goes on the list. The official list/documentation is kept by the Prosecutor's Office. Please claim your installment by reviewing or downloading the available records. If this installment has not been claimed within 30 days, or by 2/6/2026, this request will be considered abandoned and will be administratively closed as of that date. Once the available installment has been claimed, we will provide you with the estimated date of the next installment. To schedule an appointment to view the records in person at no charge, please call us at 509-477-6926. 
The Spokane County Sheriff's Office is obligated to provide records in accordance with the Washington State Public Records Act. Please note that the records you have requested may contain images and/or descriptions of events that are considered graphic in nature and may not be suitable for children or those who are sensitive to such material. 
With respect to audio recordings, please be advised that Spokane Regional Emergency Communications ("SREC") is the custodian of 911/Crime Check audio recordings as well as audio recordings of radio traffic. If you are attempting to obtain audio recordings of 911/Crime Check calls and/or radio traffic, you need to submit a separate request to SREC directly via the following link: https://srec911.com/  PLEASE NOTE: If you want to pick up printed copies of the records from the Public Safety Building, you must call at least one business day before you plan to arrive so that we can have the records ready for you. Depending on the number of pages, printing charges may apply. 509-477-6926 
Sincerely, 
JOHN F. NOWELS, Sheriff 
Spokane County 
DB 
Public Records Specialist 
Please do not update subject line above when replying.

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse” 

Our procedure is to locate records, review them for exemptions, and then prepare them for your inspection or copying. Your request was forwarded to the following Spokane County departments for review and compiling of responsive records: Prosecuting Attorney. The attached is the current PID list as you requested. 

We have not redacted or withheld any records in this installment. Because the production of documents for this installment is below the threshold for which we assess a fee for the reproduction or delivery of records, we provide this installment to you free of charge. 
After reviewing your request, we determined that clarification is necessary before we can move forward. It is unclear from your request what specific records you are attempting to obtain. Pursuant to RCW 42.56.520, we require clarification of your statement " along with accompanying impeachment material". Please clarify what specific records you are trying to obtain so that we may be able to locate responsive documents, if any. 

Until we receive clarification, we cannot act further upon your request. 
If we do not receive your clarification on or before February 16, 2026, we will consider this request to be abandoned. This means your request will be considered completed and closed because we cannot identify any responsive records. The Public Records Act provides for a one-year statute of limitations under RCW 42.56.550(6). This is the time allowed by law for you to seek judicial review. In our view, this period will begin to run on February 17, 2026 unless sufficient clarification is received. 

We look forward to receiving clarification on your public records request. 
Finally, please be advised that there may be fees associated with the reproduction or delivery of public records. Please take a moment to familiarize yourself with our Fee Schedule which is available in the FAQs. If your request requires payment, you will be sent an invoice before the records are released. . 
Sincerely, 

A Deveney 
Public Records Specialist 
509-477-5764 
Please do not update subject line above when replying.

communications.subject: [Records Center] Sheriff Public Records Request :: S026818-010526
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

Rose Terse: 
This message is to provide an update regarding your public records request assigned reference code S026818-010526. Please include this code in any communications concerning this request. 
Specifically, you requested: “To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2169967/” 
The responsive records are now available for download in the “My Request Center” of the public records portal. Instructions for downloading the files follows:  
  * Navigate to the Spokane County Public Records Center by following the blue "Public Records Center" hyperlink contained in the footer of this email. 
  * Log in with your user name and password. You will be taken directly into this request. 
  * Click on the "Download All" button directly above the blue "Files" box. *If you have a pop-up blocker, you will need to allow pop-ups for this site in order for the files to download. After they have downloaded, if the files do not open automatically, they will appear in your computer's "Downloads" folder. 
The Spokane County Sheriff's Office is obligated to provide records in accordance with the Washington State Public Records Act. Please note that the records you have requested may contain images and/or descriptions of events that are considered graphic in nature and may not be suitable for children or those who are sensitive to such material. 
This request is now considered concluded and closed because all responsive records have been provided. This is our final, definitive response to this request. Now that this request is closed, the Public Records Act's one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review has started to run because the agency does not intend to further address this request. If you have questions or concerns about this request, please contact us within 10 business days of the date of this message. If we may be of further assistance, please advise. PLEASE NOTE: If you want to pick up printed copies of the records from the Public Safety Building, you must call 509-477-6926 at least one business day before you plan to arrive so that we can have the records ready for you. Depending on the number of pages, printing charges may apply. 
Sincerely, 
JOHN F. NOWELS, Sheriff 
Spokane County 
DB 
Public Records Specialist 
Please do not update subject line above when replying.

title: Phone and text message records between police and ICE in Massachusetts (Shrewsbury Police Department)

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Phone and text message records between police and ICE in Massachusetts (Shrewsbury Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:  

1. All departmental or municipal policies regarding cell phone use and procedures for retaining cell phone and landline phone data. The records request includes, but is not limited to, policies regarding: 

*   Officer or employee use of cellphones 
*       Officer use of social media 
*       Records retention practices of phone logs and text messages 
*       Mobile device (i.e. cell phone) management 
*       Authorized software & internet usage on department-issued devices 
*       Bringing your own device 

This request covers any policies that are currently in effect or that have been in effect at any point on or after January 20, 2025. 

2. Current contract and most recent procurement records related to managing mobile phone devices within your department. This includes information about the mobile device management system (e.g. Microsoft Intune, Panasonic Connect, etc.)  that is used by the department to manage employee cell phone and computer communications, as well as screenshots of how department mobile technology is centrally managed. 

3. Call logs, if any, from January 20, 2025, to the day this request is responded to, between a department phone (including both department provided cell phones and landlines) and the following numbers: 

*   (802) 872-6020   
*   (781) 359-7500  
*   (860) 244-0522 
*   (413) 221-1303 
*   (857) 416-3742 
*   (781) 359-7451 
*   (703) 725-5982 
*   (781) 359-7553 
*   (781) 917-5143 
*   (857) 416-3705 
*   (603) 493-4520 

  Any other phone number known by the department to be a phone contact for Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or Customs and Border Patrol (CBP). 

4. Text message records from department issued cell phones (including SMS, RCS, and iMessage) communications sent or received on department issued mobile devices from January 20, 2025, to the date this request is responded to, that contain any of the following terms: 

*       ICE 
*       CBP 
*       Immigration 
*       Immigrant 
*       Detainer 

This request includes messages stored on devices themselves, in any backup system, cloud storage, mobile device management platform, or held by any third-party vendor on behalf of the department. 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as this request is in the public interest. I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available.  

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.  I am happy to discuss this request with your staff to help clarify any matters. 

 

Sincerely, 

Joshua Dankoff 
Director of Strategic Initaitives 
Citizens for Juvenile Justice 
joshuadankoff@cfjj.org  
(submitted via muckrock)

* (857) 416-3705
* (603) 493-4520

Any other phone number known by the department to be a phone contact for
Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or Customs and Border Patrol
(CBP).


4. Text message records from department issued cell phones (including SMS,
RCS, and iMessage) communications sent or received on department issued
mobile devices from January 20, 2025, to the date this request is responded
to, that contain any of the following terms:

* ICE
* CBP
* Immigration
* Immigrant
* Detainer

This request includes messages stored on devices themselves, in any backup
system, cloud storage, mobile device management platform, or held by any
third-party vendor on behalf of the department.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as this request is in
the public interest. I expect the request to be filled in an accessible
format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for
persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen
readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical
documents. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you
would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I
would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if
available.


*Here is the Department's response to your request:*

1. All departmental or municipal policies regarding cell phone use and
procedures for retaining cell phone and landline phone data. The records
request includes, but is not limited to, policies regarding:

* Officer or employee use of cellphones
* Officer use of social media
* Records retention practices of phone logs and text messages
* Mobile device (i.e. cell phone) management
* Authorized software & internet usage on department-issued devices
* Bringing your own device

This request covers any policies that are currently in effect or that have
been in effect at any point on or after January 20, 2025.

*Attached are several policies that cover employee use of cell phones,
officer use of social media and software and internet usage on department
issued devices.  Our retention practices of Department owned phone logs are
consistent with Massachusetts Records Retention policies which is 1 year.
 *

*The current cell phone policy was issued 12/8/25; The current Information
technology use policy was issued 11/21/25; The current Speech, Expression
and Social Networking Policy was issued 12/08/25.  **The other policy
attached is the 025 Computer Use Policy is the older policy that covered
software and internet usage on department issued devices prior to
11/21/25.  There was no Social networking or cell phone policy in place
prior to the current policy.     *


2. Current contract and most recent procurement records related to managing
mobile phone devices within your department. This includes information
about the mobile device management system (e.g. Microsoft Intune, Panasonic
Connect, etc.) that is used by the department to manage employee cell phone
and computer communications, as well as screenshots of how department
mobile technology is centrally managed.

*The Department has no records responsive for this request.  *

*All departmental IT systems are managed by a separate municipal entity
(SELCO IT). The Town of Shrewsbury purchases hardware and software via the
Commonwealth of Massachusetts IT47 and IT48 contracts. All other major
purchases will follow the open bid process as per Commonwealth procurement
laws.  *


3. Call logs, if any, from January 20, 2025, to the day this request is
responded to, between a department phone (including both department
provided cell phones and landlines) and the following numbers:

* (802) 872-6020
* (781) 359-7500
* (860) 244-0522
* (413) 221-1303
* (857) 416-3742
* (781) 359-7451
* (703) 725-5982
* (781) 359-7553
* (781) 917-5143
* (857) 416-3705
* (603) 493-4520

Any other phone number known by the department to be a phone contact for
Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or Customs and Border Patrol
(CBP).

 *All call logs are managed by a seperate municipal entity (SELCO IT).  I
have attached two call logs (Verizon (Text and Cell) and Zoom (Landline))
from SELCO IT.    *


4. Text message records from department issued cell phones (including SMS,
RCS, and iMessage) communications sent or received on department issued
mobile devices from January 20, 2025, to the date this request is responded
to, that contain any of the following terms:

* ICE
* CBP
* Immigration
* Immigrant
* Detainer

This request includes messages stored on devices themselves, in any backup
system, cloud storage, mobile device management platform, or held by any
third-party vendor on behalf of the department.



*The Department has no records responsive to this request. *

Please be advised that pursuant to 950 CMR 32.00 and M.G.L. c. 66, s.
10A(a) you have the right to appeal this decision to the Supervisor
of Public Records within 90 calendar days.  Such appeal must be in writing
and should include a copy of your original request and if available, this
response.  Please note that the Supervisor may only consider appeals
arising from written public records requests.  Pursuant to M.G.L. c. 66, s.
10A (c), you also have the right to seek judicial review by commencing a
civil action in Superior Court.


Should you have any questions please reach out to the Police clerk's
office.


Respectfully,

Sgt. Napolitano
--
Sergeant Larry Napolitano
Administrative Sergeant
Town of Shrewsbury
106 Maple Avenue
Shrewsbury, MA 01545
