title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Genesee County)

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Genesee County)
communications.communication:
Dear Genesee County Board of County Commissioners FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Genesee County Board of County Commissioners county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

communications.subject: [Invoice Created] Genesee County, Michigan public records request #26-538
communications.communication:
Genesee County, Michigan ************************************************************************ An invoice has been created for record request #26-538. To: Alexander Munro - MuckRock News FOIA Request #26-538 Please accept this correspondence in response to the public records request submitted to Genesee County. The records request was deemed officially recognized by me, Genesee County’s FOIA Specialist, on April 6, 2026, and it has been processed pursuant to the provisions of the Michigan Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), MCL 15.231 et seq. I understand the records request to be for a copy of “county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format for Genesee County specifically requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price.” Please know that in responding to the records request, I worked closed with and relied upon information received from the Deputy Treasurer of the Genesee County Treasurers Office. The records request is granted. The fee for providing copies of records of a County Treasurer is determined by the Transcripts and Abstracts of Records Act, MCL 48.101 (the “TARA”), MCL 15.234(4); Title Office, Inc. v. Van Buren County Treasurer, 469 Mich. 516; 676 N.W.2d 207 (2004). The TARA sets the fee as follows: “for an abstract of taxes on any description of land, or an abstract with statement of name and residence of taxpayers, $0.25, but with a minimum of $0.50 per abstract. See MCL 48.101 Section 1 (1)(a) and (b); and MCL 48.101(3).” As of March 9, 2026, there are 16,973 records responsive to the records request. At the statutory cost of $0.50 per abstract, the fee for providing these records is $8,486.50. If you wish to receive these records, please make the check/money order payable to the Genesee County Treasurer, with FOIA Request #26-538 written on the subject line, and U.S. mail it to the below address: Genesee County Board of County Commissioners Attn: Monaca Elston, Genesee County FOIA Specialist 324 S. Saginaw Street Flint, MI 48502 Upon receipt of the payment, I will inform the Genesee County Treasurers Office to finalize the processing of request. I estimate a processing time of 10-business days from receipt of payment to release the records to you through the portal. As set forth under Section 4(14) of the FOIA, MCL 15.234(14), if a fee appeal has not been filed under Section 10 of the FOIA, the Genesee County FOIA Coordinator must receive the required fee within 45 days after the statutorily-determined receipt of this notice, which is May 22, 2026, otherwise the request will be considered abandoned and Genesee County will not be required to fulfill the request. Copies of Genesee County’s FOIA Policy and Procedures, along with its FOIA Summary, are online: https://www.geneseecountymi.gov/government/freedom_of_information.php. PORTAL INSTRUCTIONS (1) Payments cannot be accepted through the portal. Please kindly submit payment as instructed above. Respectfully. Monaca Elston, Genesee County FOIA Specialist ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at Genesee County, Michigan.

communications.subject: Your Genesee County, Michigan public records request #26-538 has been closed.
communications.communication:
Genesee County, Michigan ************************************************************************ Record request #26-538 has been closed. The closure reason supplied was: To: Alexander Munro - MuckRock News Request #26-538 This correspondence is an amended response for the public records request submitted to Genesee County’s FOIA Specialist. The request was deemed originally recognized on April 6, 2026, and it has been processed pursuant to the provisions of the Michigan Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), MCL 15.231 et seq. *Genesee County offices were closed for business on Friday, April 3, 2026, for Good Friday. I understand the request to be for copies of "Genesee County Board of County Commissioners county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request. Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data." Please know that in responding to the request, I worked with and relied upon information received from Genesee County’s Equalization Director, Ms. Hayduk. The request is denied at this time. To the best of the Ms. Hayduk’s knowledge, information, and belief, under the information provided by you or by any other description reasonably known to Genesee County, the public records do not exist. A thorough search was conducted, and no responsive records were located per information received from the Ms. Hayduk, I hereby certify. In the spirit of transparency, please be advised that you may submit a new request seeking 2026 assessment data but not until after June 1, 2026, when the final State Equalization has been approved. Please know that there will be a cost associated with fulfilling the request To the extent this response constitutes a denial of the request, you have the right to submit a written appeal by sending an email to foiaappeals@geneseecountymi.gov, addressed to the chairperson of the Genesee County Board of County Commissioners, specifically stating the word “appeal” and identifying the reason or reasons for reversal of the disclosure denial. You are further advised that you have the right to seek judicial review of such denial and obtain attorney’s fees and damages to the extent provided in, and subject to the conditions of, section 10 of the FOIA (“Section 10”). A copy of Section 10 is uploaded to the portal and is incorporated in this response by reference. Copies of Genesee County’s FOIA Policy and Procedures, along with its FOIA Summary, are available online at: https://www.geneseecountymi.gov/government/freedom_of_information.php.   PORTAL INSTRUCTIONS To locate the "Section 10" document released by Genesee County through the portal, please click on the "Documents" tab located directly below the "Request" box. This is where you can view, download, and print the "Section 10" document released to you.   There was no charge for responding to this public records request. Respectfully. Monaca Elston, Genesee County FOIA Specialist ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at Genesee County, Michigan.

title: Sullivan King Contract Agreement (Associated Students Of The University Of Nevada)

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: Sullivan King Contract Agreement (Associated Students Of The University Of Nevada)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am looking for the performer contract between American artist Sullivan King and UNR/ASUN regarding his upcoming performance at the J Resort in Reno, Nevada on 4/11/26. Any documents surrounding this agreement and event are helpful.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Madison Kitch
